steps.title: Use the rate of change
steps.content:
The function is linear, and only the increase is asked for. The coefficient of $x$ tells how many Fahrenheit degrees correspond to a $1$ kelvin increase.
$$
F(x)=\frac{9}{5}(x-273.15)+32
$$

steps.title: Scale the increase
steps.content: Multiply the kelvin increase by the rate of change. $$ \frac{9}{5}(2.40)=1.8(2.40)=4.32 $$ The answer is A.
